summary: Frontend-only Playwright testing on the public preview URL for the static Next.js landing 'Expediente Jessy'. All 11 requested feature groups passed (41/41 assertions). Navbar, smooth-scroll navigation, Hero, Dossier, Timeline (6 cards), Gallery (6 items), Testimonials (4), Footer, ReportForm validation + graceful error (Formspree placeholder), and mobile hamburger menu all work. 16/16 images load with no broken images. No critical or minor frontend bugs detected.
action_items: Replace FORMSPREE_ENDPOINT placeholder 'https://formspree.io/f/REEMPLAZAR_ID' with a real Formspree form ID before production launch (currently returns 404 and renders contact-error, which is expected behavior per request).
critical_code_review_comments: ReportForm.tsx: on submit failure, the form values are preserved (good UX). Consider also keeping accessibility live region (aria-live) on contact-error/contact-success for screen readers.

Navbar.tsx: data-testid uses raw lowercase labels with accents (e.g. navbar-link-cronología, navbar-link-galería). This works but accented testids can be brittle for some tools; consider slugifying (cronologia/galeria).

Hero collage uses Image fill without an explicit container ratio relative to the parent height set in viewport units; on very small mobile the collage overlap looks fine but rotation may clip on extreme widths (<360px). Not blocking.

Console shows Next.js dev preload warnings for two woff2 font files ('preloaded but not used within a few seconds'). Cosmetic dev-only warning; can be ignored or addressed by tuning next/font display options.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static Next.js site served on port 3000 via supervisor (`yarn start`). Public URL: https://6e03ea71-7deb-400b-9760-08adb4136c5e.preview.emergentagent.com. There is NO backend (supervisor shows backend FATAL — irrelevant for this app). The contact form posts to a Formspree placeholder; the resulting 404 + visible contact-error is intentional and was validated. All data-testids documented in components/*.tsx match the request. Mobile menu data-testid is navbar-mobile-menu; opens via navbar-menu-toggle.
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA. The only console error (404 on POST to formspree.io/f/REEMPLAZAR_ID) is by design until the agency replaces the placeholder ID; the UI handles it gracefully via the 'error' status branch in ReportForm.tsx, showing data-testid='contact-error' without breaking the page.
